task: Go to en.wikipedia.org, search for 'Python programming language', and return the year it was first released.
Go to en.wikipedia.org and find the population of Tokyo. Return only the number.
Go to en.wikipedia.org, search for 'Eiffel Tower', and return its height in meters.
Go to en.wikipedia.org, navigate to the 'Featured article' link on the main page, and return the title of the featured article.
Go to en.wikipedia.org, search for 'OpenAI', and return the year the company was founded.
Go to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language) and return the name of the person who created Python.
Go to en.wikipedia.org, search for 'iPhone', and return the year the first iPhone was released.
Go to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/JavaScript and return the year JavaScript was first released.
Go to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Wide_Web and return the name of the person who invented the World Wide Web.
Go to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linux and return the year Linux was first released.
Go to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_by_population_(United_Nations) and return the name of the #1 most populous country.
Go to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/G7 and return the names of all G7 member countries.
Go to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language), find a link to the article about Guido van Rossum, navigate to it, and return his birth year.
Go to news.ycombinator.com and return the titles of the top 3 stories.
Go to news.ycombinator.com and return how many points the #1 ranked story has.
Go to news.ycombinator.com, click on 'new' to see the newest stories, and return the title of the first story.
Go to news.ycombinator.com, click on 'ask' to see Ask HN posts, and return the title of the top Ask HN post.
Go to news.ycombinator.com, navigate to page 2, and return the rank number of the first story shown on page 2.
Go to github.com/trending and return the name of the #1 trending repository today.
Go to github.com/trending and return the programming language of the #1 trending repository.
Go to github.com/trending/python and return the name of the #1 trending Python repository.
Go to github.com/microsoft/vscode and return the primary programming language used in the repository.
Go to pypi.org/project/requests and return the current latest version number.
Go to pypi.org/project/playwright and return the current latest version number.
Go to pypi.org and search for 'browser automation'. Return the name of the first result.
Go to pypi.org/project/numpy and return the current latest version number.
Go to pypi.org/project/pydantic and return the name of the project's author or maintainer.
Go to npmjs.com/package/lodash and return the current latest version number.
Go to npmjs.com/package/react and return the current latest version number.
Go to duckduckgo.com, search for 'capital of France', and return the answer.
Go to duckduckgo.com, search for 'speed of light in m/s', and return the value.
Go to duckduckgo.com, search for 'tallest mountain in the world', and return its name and height.
Go to duckduckgo.com, search for 'who invented the telephone', and return the inventor's name.
Go to duckduckgo.com, search for 'largest ocean on Earth', and return its name.
Go to duckduckgo.com, search for 'boiling point of water in Celsius', and return the value.
Go to wttr.in/London and return the current temperature shown for London.
Go to arxiv.org, search for 'attention is all you need', and return the number of authors of the first result.
Go to arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762 and return the full title of the paper.
Go to arxiv.org/abs/1810.04805 and return the title of the paper.
Go to developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/map and return what the map() method returns according to the page.
Go to developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/display and return the first value listed under the 'Formal syntax' or check if the page describes what the display property does.
Go to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence, scroll to the bottom of the page, and return the last section heading you find.
Go to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language), scroll down until you find the 'Typing' section, and return the first sentence or description you find there.
Go to en.wikipedia.org, search for 'Claude Shannon', find when he was born, then search for 'Alan Turing' and find when he was born. Return both birth years.
Go to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language), find who created Python, then navigate to that person's Wikipedia article and return their birth year.
Go to pypi.org/project/requests and note the latest version. Then go to pypi.org/project/httpx and note its latest version. Return both version numbers.
Go to news.ycombinator.com and find the #1 story. Then go to the comments page for that story and return the total number of comments.
Go to stackoverflow.com/questions?tab=votes and return the title of the highest voted question of all time.
Go to stackoverflow.com, search for 'python list comprehension', and return the title of the first search result.
Go to news.ycombinator.com and count how many stories are listed on the front page. Return the number.
Go to en.wikipedia.org/wiki/G7 and return how many member countries the G7 has.
